channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Sanders: Trump will be 'closer to Netanyahu' on Gaza if reelected
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.) said Sunday he believes former President Trump will have similar views of the Israel-Hamas war to those of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu if he is reelected.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Cubans struggle as power not fully restored days after island-wide blackout
item.description: Many Cubans are waiting in anguish as electricity on much of the island has yet to be totally restored days after a blackout.
item.dc:creator: Milexsy Duran, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Encircling Taiwan was a smokescreen for China's real goal of convincing US not to intervene, expert says
item.description: The Joint Sword exercises used more military hardware and got closer to Taiwan than ever, but some experts say China’s bigger plan is dissuading the U.S. from even attempting to come to Taiwan’s aid.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Reddit user discovers mysterious 'doorway' in Antarctica
item.description: "Monsters Inc." bedroom doors, and an underground bunker were more suggested explanations, some in jest others not.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Indonesia swears in ex-general Prabowo Subianto as president
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — Prabowo Subianto was inaugurated Sunday as the eighth president of the world’s most populous Muslim-majority nation, completing his journey from an ex-general accused of rights abuses during the dark days of Indonesia’s military dictatorship to the presidential palace.
item.dc:creator: NINIEK KARMINI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Cuba gets some electricity back after major power outage left millions in the dark
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — Some electricity was restored in Cuba, the government said Saturday, after the island nation's worst blackout in at least two years left millions without electricity for two days.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Children greet King Charles III and Queen Camilla outside a Sydney church
item.description: SYDNEY (AP) — King Charles III and Queen Camilla were greeted by children Sunday outside a Sydney church in their first public appearance of their Australian visit.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Guatemalan judge grants investigative journalist Zamora house arrest and his family celebrates
item.description: GUATEMALA CITY (AP) — The family of a Guatemalan journalist on Saturday celebrated his transfer to house arrest following his jailing for more than two years amid his daily newspaper's anti-corruption investigations.
item.dc:creator: SONIA PEREZ D., Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: What to know about the electrical grid failure that plunged Cuba into darkness
item.description: A few things to know after millions of people in Cuba were left without electricity for two days when the nation's electrical grid collapsed.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Rodriguez, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ukraine’s ‘victory plan’ receives mixed reactions from Western allies
item.description: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy’s plan to end Ukraine’s nearly three-year war with Russia is getting mixed reactions from Western allies.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Arhirova, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: France vows support for Ukraine's plan to end Russian invasion
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — France's foreign minister pledged his support for Ukraine’s plan for ending the war with Russia, telling reporters in Kyiv on Saturday that he would work with Ukrainian officials to secure other nations’ backing for the proposal.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The first migrants sent to Albania for processing return to Italy
item.description: SHENGJIN, Albania (AP) — An Italian navy ship on Saturday took back to Italy the first 12 migrants from newly opened asylum processing centers in Albania following a contentious court decision in Rome.
item.dc:creator: VLASOV SULAJ and LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel says drone launched toward Netanyahu's home from Lebanon
item.description: The office of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said early Saturday that a drone was launched from Lebanon toward Netanyahu's home in Caesarea. Netanyahu and his wife were not home at the time of the incident and no casualties were reported, according to The Associated Press (AP).
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: A drone targets the Israeli prime minister's house during new barrages with Hezbollah
item.description: Israel’s government says a drone targeted Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s house on Saturday, with no casualties.
item.dc:creator: Adam Schreck And Samy Magdy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Belgrade's landmark Hotel Yugoslavia faces likely demolition, with many opposed
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Belgrade’s iconic Hotel Yugoslavia, once a symbol of progress in the former socialist state of Yugoslavia that broke apart in the 1990s and a favorite gathering place for local residents as well as world leaders, now stands in eerie silence awaiting its likely demolition.
item.dc:creator: DUSAN STOJANOVIC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Hezbollah says its war with Israel is entering a new phase
item.description: Lebanon’s Hezbollah militant group said Friday it is entering a new phase in its fight against invading Israeli troops, as the region reckons with the killing of top Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar in a battle with Israeli forces in Gaza. Hamas acknowledged Sinwar’s death and described him as a martyr.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Cuba’s grid goes offline with massive blackout after a major power plant fails
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — Cuba’s electrical grid went down Friday after one of the island’s major power plants failed, a day after a massive blackout swept across the Caribbean island and with no official estimate for when service will be restored.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRÍGUEZ, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Cuban power plant failure leaves millions in the dark in island-wide outage
item.description: Millions of Cubans were left without power on Friday after a failure at a power plant, the communist government said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Palau’s vibrant Helen Reef is a magnet for poachers. These rangers keep it safe
item.description: HELEN ISLAND, Palau (AP) — The handful of rangers who protect one of Earth's most remote and biologically diverse reefs have only each other for company for months at a time.
item.dc:creator: YANNICK PETERHANS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cuba's power grid fails, plunging country into darkness
item.description: Cuba's government closed schools and non-essential industries and sent most state workers home in a last-ditch effort to keep the lights on for residents.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Two Venezuelan men shot in head in Juarez
item.description: Police in Juarez, Mexico, are investigating a shooting that left one Venezuelan dead, and another critically injured on Wednesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: IDF video: Israeli tank fires at building where Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar was barricaded before being killed
item.description: A building where Hamas chief Yahya Sinwar had been barricaded was targeted by Israeli tank fire before the terror leader was killed on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sinwar's ruthless brother Mohammed slated to take over as leader of Hamas
item.description: Mohammed Sinwar is expected to take over as leader of Hamas after his older brother Yahya Sinwar was confirmed to have been killed in Gaza by the Israel Defense Forces as the brutal war continues.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean intelligence says North has sent troops to aid Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea's spy agency said Friday that North Korea has dispatched troops to support Russia's war against Ukraine. If confirmed, the move would bring a third country into the war and intensify a standoff between North Korea and the West.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Military voters see 'direct attack' from GOP election challenges
item.description: Republicans are coming under fire from veterans and military groups for attempting to restrict the votes of overseas service members through a series of lawsuits in battleground states.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Hamas terror leader Yahya Sinwar killed in Gaza, Israel says
item.description: Israeli officials announced Thursday that Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar was killed during military operations inside the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: An Australian police sergeant likely to be charged over a Nazi salute
item.description: An Australian state police chief apologized to the Jewish community after a sergeant allegedly performed a Nazi salute.
item.dc:creator: Rod McGuirk, The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli strike in northern Lebanon kills at least 21 people
item.description: AITO, Lebanon (AP) — An Israeli airstrike hit an apartment building in northern Lebanon on Monday, killing at least 21 people, according to the Lebanese Red Cross. The Israeli military did not immediately comment and the target was not clear.
item.dc:creator: YAHYA HABCHITI, KAREEM CHEHAYEB, and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: U.S. to send troops and air defense system to Israel
item.description: The Pentagon announced the U.S. military will send a Terminal High Altitude Defense battery and about 100 U.S. troops to Israel.
item.dc:creator: Vinay Simlot, WGN 9

item.title: Vial linked to UK woman's death had enough poison to kill thousands
item.description: Sturgess and her partner collapsed after they came into contact with a discarded perfume bottle containing the nerve agent.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: North Korea is preparing to destroy northern parts of inter-Korean roads, Seoul says
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea said Monday it has detected signs that North Korea is preparing to destroy the northern parts of inter-Korean roads no longer in use, as the rivals are embroiled in soaring tensions over North Korea’s claim that South Korea flew drones over its territory.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation
